entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The legume crop studied for its growth, development, biomass accumulation, and partitioning.

entities.name: BIOMASS ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The process of increasing plant biomass through radiation interception and use efficiency.

entities.name: BIOMASS PARTITIONING
entities.description: The distribution of accumulated biomass between different plant organs such as leaves, stems, and grains.

entities.name: RADIATION EXTINCTION COEFFICIENT
entities.description: A coefficient (k) quantifying the rate at which radiation is absorbed by the plant canopy.

entities.name: RADIATION USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: A measure (RUE) of the efficiency with which intercepted radiation is converted into biomass.

entities.name: HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of grain yield to total above-ground biomass (HI).

entities.name: WATER AND NUTRIENT NON-LIMITING CONDITIONS
entities.description: Experimental conditions where water and nutrients are provided in sufficient quantities to avoid growth limitation.

entities.name: ICRISAT CENTRE
entities.description: The research facility located in Patancheru, Andhra Pradesh, India where the study was conducted.

entities.name: CULTIVAR DURATION CLASSES
entities.description: Classification of pigeonpea cultivars based on maturity time: extra-short, short, and medium.

entities.name: INDETERMINATE GROWTH
entities.description: A growth pattern where vegetative growth continues concurrently with reproductive growth.

entities.name: POD-FILLING
entities.description: The developmental stage of the crop during which seeds in the pods accumulate dry matter.

entities.name: INTERCEPTION OF RADIATION
entities.description: The process by which the plant canopy captures solar radiation.
